title: Kong passes Authorization header through to PostgREST
description: GET /rest/v1/ through Kong with a Bearer token in the Authorization header. PostgREST must receive the header and respond — not with a Kong error. If PostgREST returns 401 (invalid token) that is acceptable: it means Kong forwarded the header and PostgREST tried to validate it. A 200 means the token is valid. Only a Kong-level error (502, 503) or missing route (404) indicates a gateway problem.
author: dlesieur
notes: The token is intentionally invalid. We are testing that Kong passes it through to PostgREST (which rejects it with 401 + JWT error). If Kong strips the header, PostgREST would return a different error (no auth). If Kong blocks the request itself, we'd get a 403 from Kong. The 401 with 'JWT' in the body proves the header reached PostgREST.
